mnemonics.title: DBMS Advantages Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: I CAN SPEAR DICE - Independence, Consistency, Abstraction, Normalization, Security, Performance, Efficiency, Access, Recovery, Data Integrity, Integrity Constraints, Concurrency, Efficiency

acronyms.usage: Four properties that transactions must satisfy to ensure reliable database operations
Software that provides interface between users and physical database
Commands to define database schema like CREATE, ALTER, DROP
Commands to manipulate data like SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
Commands for security like GRANT, REVOKE

visualTips.visualization: Think of a building: Internal Schema = Foundation (physical storage), Conceptual Schema = Blueprints (logical structure), External Schema = Different views from windows (user perspective)
Imagine wearing different colored glasses - the world (data) stays the same, but your view (application) changes without changing the world itself
Think of multiple people writing on a whiteboard with different colored markers - they can write simultaneously but need rules to avoid erasing each other's work
Think of auto-save in a document - even if the computer crashes, you recover to the last saved state

analogies.analogy: Like a well-organized library (DBMS) vs scattered books everywhere (file processing). Library has catalog, organized shelves, and librarians to help find and manage books efficiently.
Like driving a car - you use steering wheel and pedals (external view) without needing to understand engine mechanics (internal view).
Like changing furniture arrangement in a room - the room structure (application) stays the same even though you move things around (physical storage).
